name: Stangeria
description: [FILL IN] Stangeria is a monotypic genus containing only Stangeria eriopus, the sole member of the family Stangeriaceae. It is endemic to South Africa and Mozambique and is unusual among cycads in having fern-like leaves that caused it to be mistakenly classified as a fern upon its original discovery. It is a small, largely subterranean cycad of coastal grasslands and forests.
etymology: [FILL IN] Named in honour of Dr William Stanger (1811–1854), first Surveyor-General of Natal, South Africa.

conservation_status.notes: [FILL IN] Listed as Vulnerable by the IUCN; threatened by habitat loss and collection for traditional medicine (muti).

distribution.habitat: [FILL IN] Coastal grassland, scrub forest, and forest margins; usually on sandy or loamy soils.

morphology.height_m: [FILL IN] Leaves typically 0.5–1.5 m long; stem mostly below ground.
morphology.stem: [FILL IN] Mostly subterranean; carrot-like tuberous root-stem.
morphology.leaves: [FILL IN] Pinnately compound; uniquely fern-like in appearance with softly textured, undulate leaflets; midrib conspicuous.
morphology.reproductive_structures: [FILL IN] Dioecious; cones emerge at ground level. Male cones elongated; female cones ovoid.
morphology.seeds_or_spores: [FILL IN] Seeds red when ripe, with fleshy sarcotesta.
morphology.roots: [FILL IN] Coralloid roots with nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria.

ecology.seed_dispersal: [FILL IN] Vertebrate-dispersed via fleshy sarcotesta; mongooses and other small mammals recorded.
ecology.symbioses: [FILL IN] Nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria in coralloid roots.

uses.cultural: [FILL IN] Used in traditional Zulu medicine (umTholo); roots used as protective charms and medicine.
uses.medicinal: [FILL IN] Roots used in traditional medicine; harvesting has depleted wild populations.
uses.horticultural: [FILL IN] Grown by enthusiasts; requires warm, humid conditions.

cultivation.soil: [FILL IN] Well-drained, sandy to loamy soil with organic matter.
cultivation.light: [FILL IN] Partial shade (forest form) to full sun (grassland form).
cultivation.watering: [FILL IN] Regular moisture; tolerates brief drought.
cultivation.notes: [FILL IN] CITES Appendix II; exists in two ecotypes (grassland and forest).

notable_species.notes: [FILL IN] The only species in the genus; two distinct ecotypes recognised (grassland and forest forms).
